title: Asymptotic Total Ergodicity and Polynomial Patterns in Finite Fields
speaker: Vitaly Bergelson
abstract: A version of the polynomial Szemer´edi theorem was shown to hold in finite fields in [BLM05]. In particular, one has patterns ${x, x + P1(n), . . . , x + Pk(n)}$ (1) for polynomials with zero constant term in large subsets of finite fields. When the polynomials Pi(n) are independent, one can get a substantial refinement regarding the size of subsets in which the patterns (1) can be found. This was done by Peluse ([P19]) for finite fields of sufficiently large characteristic.
tags: Workshop on Dynamics, Discrete Analysis and Multiplicative Number Theory

proof_stages.label: Classical polynomial recurrence and polynomial Szemerédi motivation
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins from Sárközy-type difference-set recurrence, its ergodic formulation, Szemerédi recurrence, and the polynomial Szemerédi theorem, introducing p-rich sets and intersectivity as the initial recurrence paradigm.

proof_stages.label: Finite-field polynomial Szemerédi from module recurrence
proof_stages.summary: The Bergelson-Leibman-McCutcheon extension to modules over integral domains is used to formulate a finite-field polynomial Szemerédi theorem for dense subsets, with zero constant term or intersectivity supplying the non-independent recurrence condition.

proof_stages.label: High-characteristic asymptotic total ergodicity
proof_stages.summary: For prime fields and more generally cyclic rings with large least prime factor, polynomial shifts are explained by an asymptotic version of total ergodicity, giving quantitative convergence of polynomial correlation averages to the expected product.

proof_stages.label: Sumset consequences in large prime fields
proof_stages.summary: The single-recurrence asymptotic-total-ergodic principle implies that for two positive-density sets A and B in a large prime field, A+B contains prescribed polynomial values, yielding classical finite-field additive consequences.

proof_stages.label: Multiple recurrence via joint ergodicity and independence
proof_stages.summary: Independent polynomial iterates of a totally ergodic transformation are jointly ergodic, and quantitative van der Corput/PET induction transfers this to finite fields and rings, allowing different sets, nonzero constant terms, correct statistics, and savings under independence.

proof_stages.label: Fixed-characteristic function-field framework
proof_stages.summary: When the characteristic p is fixed and the field size grows, the integer model is replaced by F_p[t] and its rational-function/Laurent-series apparatus, and ordinary independence is refined to irrational-equidistribution-goodness to handle p-power obstructions.

proof_stages.label: Quasirandomness analogy and ring-level outlook
proof_stages.summary: The speaker frames asymptotic total ergodicity as parallel to quasirandom groups or asymptotic weak mixing, then points to extensions and open questions for low-characteristic rings and related algebraic settings.
